image.alt: Album painting of a seated woman in an elaborate golden headdress and an embroidered robe

attribution.fileTitle: File:A Tang Dynasty Empress Wu Zetian.JPG
attribution.author: Unknown author

rounds.revealFact: Wu Zetian rose from imperial consort to become the only woman ever to rule China in her own name as emperor, founding her own short-lived dynasty.

image.alt: Painting of hundreds of oar-driven galleys crowded together and firing on one another in a wide bay

clues: A vast fleet of oar-driven galleys clashed in a gulf, one of the last great sea battles decided mainly by ramming and boarding.
An alliance of Christian states broke the naval momentum of a great empire, even though the war changed few borders.

events.label: Sargon of Akkad forges the world's first empire in Mesopotamia
The Maurya Empire unites most of the Indian subcontinent

eventQuestion.prompt: What is shown in this photograph?

rounds.prompt: The volcano Krakatoa erupts, one of the most violent eruptions in recorded history.
rounds.revealFact: The 1883 eruption of Krakatoa was heard thousands of miles away and threw enough ash into the sky to cool global temperatures for months.

rounds.prompt: Where did the kingdom of Kush raise clusters of steep, narrow royal pyramids, long after the age of the great Egyptian pyramids had ended?
rounds.revealFact: Meroë was a royal city of the Kingdom of Kush; its field of more than forty small, steep-sided pyramids still stands in the Sudanese desert.
